item.title: Java FFM - Graph Storage PoC
item.description: Graph Storage PoC FFM Basics - Memory The main building blocks for foreign memory handling are Arena, MemoryLayout and MemorySegmet.

item.title: Video Fingerprinting
item.description: Intro In this post I want to share my experiments on how to generate video fingerprints.

item.title: Day4 - Vert.x jOOQ PoC
item.description: Intro In this post I want to present a reactive database layer proof of concept implementation I did which will hopefully make its way into my MetaLoom project.

item.title: Day3 - Vert.x Dagger PoC
item.description: Dagger / Vert.x In this post I want to show a proof of concept project that makes use of Eclipse Vert.

item.title: Day2 - Project Setup
item.description: Disclaimer Coke vs. Sprite Ketchup vs. Majo Eclipse vs. IntelliJ Java vs. Kotlin vs. Rust

item.title: Day1 - Project Design
item.description: Basics For Loom I knew what kind of building blocks were required since it is basically just a continuation of my work on Mesh.

item.title: Day0 - Let there be Loom
item.description: What is Loom? Loom is a headless media asset management server platform. It provides tools to inventory your media assets and extract the most information from them.

item.title: Administration Guide
item.description: Docs A B C The docs

item.title: Artifacts
item.description: Cortex publishes JVM modules and container deployment assets separately. Maven Artifacts See Maven Artifacts for the BOM-backed module list and node dependencies.

item.title: Artifacts
item.description: Loom publishes JVM artifacts and container deployment assets separately. Maven Artifacts See Maven Artifacts for the BOM and the primary client and shared model modules.

item.title: Authentication
item.description: Loom uses JWT (JSON Web Token) bearer tokens for API authentication. OAuth2 integration is also available.

item.title: CLI Arguments & Env Settings
item.description: Cortex is configured entirely via CLI arguments and environment variables. Environment variables can be used as drop-in replacements for CLI flags, which is convenient for container-based deployments.

item.title: Configuration
item.description: Loom is configured via a YAML file (loom.yml) with optional environment variable overrides. Config File Locations Loom looks for loom.

item.title: Containers
item.description: Running with Docker docker run --rm \ -e LOOM_HOST=loom.internal \ -e LOOM_PORT=7733 \ -v /media/assets:/data \ metaloom/cortex:latest \ process run /data Server Mode docker run -d \ --name cortex \ -p 8093:8093 \ -e LOOM_HOST=loom.

item.title: Containers
item.description: The Loom server is distributed as a Docker image. Running with Docker docker run -d \ --name loom \ -p 8092:8092 \ -p 8091:8091 \ -p 8989:8989 \ -e LOOM_DB_HOST=postgres \ -e LOOM_DB_PASSWORD=secret \ -e LOOM_INITIAL_PASSWORD=admin123 \ -v loom-config:/app/config \ metaloom/loom:latest Docker Compose version: "3.

item.title: Examples
item.description: The examples/ directory in the source repository contains two Cortex example projects. cortex-custom-node A minimal Maven project that shows how to build a custom processing node.

item.title: Examples
item.description: The /examples directory of the source repository contains runnable example projects. Listing Examples The examples/ Maven module contains sub-projects for both Loom and Cortex use cases.

item.title: Features
item.description: Media Processing Cortex can process any media file type — images, videos, audio, and documents.

item.title: Features
item.description: Loom provides a comprehensive set of features for managing digital media assets and their associated metadata.

item.title: Helm Chart
item.description: The Loom Helm chart is the Kubernetes deployment entry point for running the server in-cluster.

item.title: Java Client Usage
item.description: The loom-rest-client module provides a typed HTTP client for JVM applications. Maven Dependency <dependency> <groupId>io.

item.title: Johannes Schüth
item.description: Johannes main focus is backend development. But he does not mind devops and the occasional frontend development.

item.title: Loom & Cortex Interaction
item.description: Loom and Cortex are complementary components that can be deployed together or used independently.

item.title: Maven Artifacts
item.description: All Cortex artifacts are published under the io.metaloom group and should generally be consumed with the shared BOM.

item.title: Maven Artifacts
item.description: All Loom artifacts are published under the io.metaloom group. BOM POM Use the BOM to align versions across Loom and Cortex modules:

item.title: Monitoring API
item.description: Cortex exposes a lightweight monitoring/health endpoint that can be used by load balancers, Kubernetes liveness probes, and Prometheus scrapers.

item.title: Pipeline Mechanism
item.description: A pipeline is a directed acyclic graph (DAG) of processing nodes that Cortex executes on each media asset.

item.title: Pipeline Nodes
item.description: Cortex ships with a set of built-in processing nodes. Each node has a unique name that is used to reference it in CLI --actions flags and pipeline definitions.

item.title: REST API
item.description: Loom exposes its full functionality via a versioned REST API. All endpoints are rooted at /api/v1.

item.title: REST API
item.description: Tip Try the example https://demo.metaloom.io/api/v1/demo/assets API endpoints Description API endpoint Get the asset with the given UUID.
